title: Drop default log-level from error to warn
body:
Our users are missing certain warning messages that would
make debugging issues with Podman easier.

For example if you do a podman build with a Containerfile
that contains the SHELL directive, the Derective is silently
ignored.

If you run with the log-level warn you get a warning message explainging
what happened.

$ podman build --no-cache -f /tmp/Containerfile1 /tmp/
STEP 1: FROM ubi8
STEP 2: SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
STEP 3: COMMIT
--> 7a207be102a
7a207be102aa8993eceb32802e6ceb9d2603ceed9dee0fee341df63e6300882e

$ podman --log-level=warn build --no-cache -f /tmp/Containerfile1 /tmp/
STEP 1: FROM ubi8
STEP 2: SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
STEP 3: COMMIT
WARN[0000] SHELL is not supported for OCI image format, [/bin/bash -c] will be ignored. Must use `docker` format
--> 7bd96fd25b9
7bd96fd25b9f755d8a045e31187e406cf889dcf3799357ec906e90767613e95f

These messages will no longer be lost, when we default to WARNing level.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8477

We might have to lower some Warning messages to Info or Debug, if they are not useful to the users.
Will see what the CI system finds.
Buildah currently defaults to Warn level.

Please do not merge this before 2.2 is cut. This is definitely a 3.0 change.

Agreed, which is why I set the flag.

Need https://github.com/cri-o/ocicni/pull/82 merged before we can move on with this.
Basically we need to make sure vendored applications do not Warn, when there is nothing the user can do about it.
LGTM assuming happy tests

@mheon This change is causing some strange test differences.  
In logs_test "streaming output" test.

The container ends up in the "stopped" state rather then the "exited" state.  Any idea why a change of 
loglevel would cause this problem?

It seems like whatever you did managed to break the cleanup process - the
container is probably not being unmounted either. Maybe a bad CLI argument
to said cleanup process? Ed wrote a script that intercepted the call to the
cleanup process and printed the arguments it was called with which could
help?

On Fri, Nov 27, 2020, 08:41 Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> @mheon <https://github.com/mheon> This change is causing some strange
> test differences.
> In logs_test "streaming output" test.
>
> The container ends up in the "stopped" state rather then the "exited"
> state. Any idea why a change of
> loglevel would cause this problem?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8479#issuecomment-734840689>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCGMX76V6FTYH7FAV6TSR6UBLANCNFSM4UCOW7CQ>
> .
>
I am seeing it locally also.  Will see if I can execute the cleanup command and if it fails.

FWIW this fails root system tests on my laptop, lots of unexpected warnings:
```
time="2020-11-30T07:49:39-07:00" level=warning msg="Failed to add conmon to systemd sandbox cgroup: Unit libpod-conmon-3adb97f2b360f615f1bff55a0753624132f4ad5a551ceec87dc5504795c85a28.scope already exists."
```
and
```
time="2020-11-30T07:50:06-07:00" level=warning msg="Timed out stopping container 02c95917b41829234935840639cd69e5330922ae4336ff07cdc8fd82d5f89462, resorting to SIGKILL"
```

The e2e test failures were caused on the cleanup generation.  
Basically
podman --log-level warn ...

Would generate a
podman --log-level warning container cleanup.

Then the cleanup line would silently fail,because "warning" was not accepted by the CLI.  This is now fixed.

@edsantiago do you think we should just igore the warnings.  These seem to be valid warnings, that we have not been exposing to users up until now.  Perhaps we should drop these warnings back to Info if there is nothing the user can do about them, and they do not cause breakage?
The stop one should definitely drop to Info.

If my tests are suddenly seeing warnings that were never there before, then end users will suddenly start seeing warnings that were never there before. I would expect some/many of those to react with concern, and to waste time investigating, and to be upset when it turns out to be just an artifact of log level.

System tests are going to fail. On my system (f32) I'm still seeing:
```console
$ sudo ./bin/podman run -d --rm --name foo alpine sleep 20
9d9677fa601d46a99da0ed72ee3e5e7bea794e61c456243ca957ceec0ef27a8a
$ sudo ./bin/podman exec foo true
WARN[0000] Failed to add conmon to systemd sandbox cgroup: Unit libpod-conmon-9d9677fa601d46a99da0ed72ee3e5e7bea794e61c456243ca957ceec0ef27a8a.scope already exists.
```
I see this only as root, never as rootless. On the previous CI run, I saw failures in rootless but not root. That concerns me. And I really don't think users need or want to see this.

Looking at [int podman f32 root container](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5182224708403200/html/int-podman-fedora-32-root-container.log.html) log, I see a lot of:
```
time="2020-11-30T16:23:35Z" level=warning msg="Error updating pod 363314e20620c674476220359e0e0c13cc03412c35136922eae4adbe9cc24d30 conmon cgroup /libpod_parent/363314e20620c674476220359e0e0c13cc03412c35136922eae4adbe9cc24d30/conmon PID limit: open /sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/363314e20620c674476220359e0e0c13cc03412c35136922eae4adbe9cc24d30/conmon/pids.max: no such file or directory"
```
@giuseppe Is this expected?  IE Should I drop the Warning to Info?

@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL
@edsantiago @mheon PTAL
/lgtm
No changes in tests? LGTM!
